title: Fourier Decoupling — Bourgain–Demeter ℓ² Decoupling Proof DAG
description: Vertical dependency tower in harmonic analysis: multilinear Kakeya (Guth) → Bourgain–Demeter ℓ² decoupling → {Vinogradov main conjecture (Bourgain–Demeter–Guth), subconvexity for ζ(1/2+it) (Bourgain)} → large-value estimates / zero density / primes in short intervals (Guth–Maynard). Each layer consumes the previous as a black box plus genuinely new ideas — the structural property a typed proof DAG exposes that a citation graph flattens. Built root-down from 1403.5335.

1409.4683.title: A short proof of the multilinear Kakeya inequality
1409.4683.role: upstream-root — the multilinear Kakeya/Loomis–Whitney input both decoupling proofs rest on

1403.5335.title: The proof of the l^2 Decoupling Conjecture
1403.5335.role: root — sharp ℓ² decoupling for curved hypersurfaces and the cone

1408.5794.title: Decoupling, exponential sums and the Riemann zeta function
1408.5794.role: application — decoupling ⟹ exponential-sum bound ⟹ |ζ(1/2+it)| ≪ t^{13/84+ε}; the bridge into analytic number theory

1512.01565.title: Proof of the main conjecture in Vinogradov's mean value theorem for degrees higher than three
1512.01565.role: flagship-application — Vinogradov's mean value theorem in the conjectured sharp range, via moment-curve decoupling proved by induction on degree on the parabola base case

2405.20552.title: New large value estimates for Dirichlet polynomials
2405.20552.role: downstream-sibling — new zero-density estimate and primes in short intervals of length x^{17/30+ε}; methodologically orthogonal (singular-value/trace method) but in the same problem space as the decoupling-derived exponential-sum line

related_dags.large-values-zero-density: Shares the nodes 1408.5794 (Bourgain's decoupling $\zeta$-bound) and 2405.20552 (Guth–Maynard). This DAG carries the upstream story (multilinear Kakeya $\to$ $\ell^2$ decoupling $\to$ Bourgain's $\zeta$-curve decoupling $\to$ exponential-sum bound); the large-values DAG carries the downstream story (large values $\to$ zero density $\to$ primes). Together they trace decoupling techniques to prime distribution.
related_dags.kakeya-restriction: Shares the node math/0509262 (Bennett–Carbery–Tao, multilinear restriction and Kakeya). Here its multilinear restriction (thm:1.16) is imported by the $\ell^2$ decoupling theorem (1403.5335:thm:6.1) and its multilinear Kakeya (thm:1.15) is improved by Guth (0811.2251:thm:2); in the kakeya-restriction DAG the same multilinear restriction is the input to Guth's restriction estimate (2402.03470:thm:3.1). Multilinear Kakeya is a load-bearing hub on which both the decoupling and the restriction programs depend.

1012.3760.title: Bounds on oscillatory integral operators based on multilinear estimates
1012.3760.reason: Applies Bennett–Carbery–Tao multilinear restriction to the Stein restriction conjecture / Hörmander oscillatory integrals / Bochner–Riesz — a sibling of the decoupling line, not an ancestor of it. Natural root of a separate 'restriction-conjecture' DAG. Also currently un-parseable (latex2json AST-extraction gap for this pre-2014 source).

BennettCarberyTao:2006.title: On the multilinear restriction and Kakeya conjectures
BennettCarberyTao:2006.role: foundational — multilinear restriction/Kakeya, the geometric input behind both decoupling proofs

Guth:2010Endpoint.title: The endpoint case of the Bennett-Carbery-Tao theorem
Guth:2010Endpoint.role: foundational — endpoint multilinear Kakeya, later re-proved short in 1409.4683

BCCT:BrascampLieb.title: Finite bounds for Hölder-Brascamp-Lieb multilinear inequalities
BCCT:BrascampLieb.role: foundational — Brascamp–Lieb finiteness criterion used by BDG

Wolff:2000.title: Local smoothing type estimates on L^p for large p
Wolff:2000.role: foundational — first lᵖ decoupling (for the cone)

TomasStein:Restriction.title: Tomas–Stein restriction estimate
TomasStein:Restriction.role: foundational — the continuous restriction prototype decoupling sharpens

Huxley:ExpSums.title: Exponential sums and the Riemann zeta function V
Huxley:ExpSums.role: foundational — the exponential-sum / zero-density baseline improved by 1408.5794 and 2405.20552

HeathBrown:LargeValues.title: A large values estimate for Dirichlet polynomials
HeathBrown:LargeValues.role: foundational — classical large-values bound that 2405.20552 builds on and surpasses

LoomisWhitney:1949.title: An inequality related to the isoperimetric inequality
LoomisWhitney:1949.role: foundational — base case of the multilinear Kakeya induction
